title: PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS - 211info
text: CONSUMERS POWER INC LEBANON OFFICE PUBLIC SAFETY POWER SHUTOFFS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) is an operational practice an electric utility may use to shut off power in high-risk areas to help prevent wildfires during extreme and dangerous weather conditions. PSPS means Consumers Power Inc (CPI) will shut off power, and it is considered an action of last resort to help protect public safety. Additionally, as a wholesale power customer of the Bonneville Power Administration (BPA), CPI may also be subject to a PSPS initiated by BPA. 1900 West Oak Street Lebanon, OR 97355 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 929-3124 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (541) 929-3124 Main phone (800) 872-9036 Toll-free Main phone (541) 929-3124 Toll-free (800) 872-9036 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Benton, Lane, Lincoln, Linn, Marion and Polk Counties Services: Electric Service Connection/Repair * Disaster/Emergency Issues If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
